description_long: This object is a botanical specimen featuring a cluster of blue, trumpet-shaped flowers arranged at the top of a green, leafy stem. The flowers have a radial symmetry, with petals that are slightly fused at the base and flare outward, creating a star-like appearance. The petals are a vibrant blue with subtle gradients, and the flower centers are lighter, possibly with visible stamens. The stem is upright, slender, and green, supporting several lanceolate leaves that are alternately arranged and have a smooth texture. The overall structure is elongated, with the flowers forming a terminal inflorescence at the top of the stem. The object is naturalistic and would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped areas.
description: A cluster of blue, trumpet-shaped flowers with lanceolate green leaves on a slender stem, forming a terminal inflorescence. The petals are radially arranged and the overall appearance is vibrant and naturalistic.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main cluster of blue flowers prominently, with the green stem and several leaves visible below.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the two main flower clusters and shows the color calibration cube partially obscured by the stem.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the blue flowers from a different angle, with the stem and leaves more visible and the calibration cube to the left.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the back of the flower clusters and the arrangement of the leaves along the stem, with the calibration cube mostly hidden.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged radially in a whorled configuration, with each flower displaying a star-like symmetry. The petals are slightly fused at the base and overlap minimally, creating a trumpet-shaped corolla typical of many Gentianaceae species.
